Agent Intellect (Nous Poietikos).title: Generation of Animals
Agent Intellect (Nous Poietikos).source: referenced in discussions of nous thurathen
Agent Intellect (Nous Poietikos).abstract: Aristotle's biological treatise examining reproduction and embryology, containing the famous statement that intellect (nous) alone 'comes from outside' (thurathen) and is divine
Agent Intellect (Nous Poietikos).seminal_reason: Introduced the concept that nous 'comes from without' (thurathen)—generated centuries of debate about whether human rational intellect has transcendent origin or emerges from natural processes

Agent Intellect (Nous Poietikos).title: On the Soul
Agent Intellect (Nous Poietikos).source: referenced in 'Nous thurathen: between Theophrastus and Alexander of Aphrodisias'
Agent Intellect (Nous Poietikos).abstract: Theophrastus' commentary and elaboration on Aristotle's psychology, wrestling with the problem of nous thurathen—early interpretation shaping subsequent debates on rational soul's origin
Agent Intellect (Nous Poietikos).seminal_reason: Aristotle's successor's first systematic wrestling with nous thurathen problem—initiated the interpretive tradition struggling with whether rational intellect is natural or transcendent

Agent Intellect (Nous Poietikos).title: On the Intellect
Agent Intellect (Nous Poietikos).source: referenced in 'Nous thurathen: between Theophrastus and Alexander of Aphrodisias'
Agent Intellect (Nous Poietikos).abstract: Alexander's influential interpretation of Aristotle's agent intellect as God—establishing influential reading that nous poietikos is divine and external to individual human souls
Agent Intellect (Nous Poietikos).seminal_reason: Interpreted agent intellect (nous poietikos) as external divine mind—profoundly influenced medieval debates on whether human rationality requires divine illumination

Agrarian Scale And Farm Size Economics.title: The Role of Agriculture in Economic Development
Agrarian Scale And Farm Size Economics.abstract: Early treatise on agriculture's function in economic development, examining how agricultural organization and productivity shape broader economic transformation
Agrarian Scale And Farm Size Economics.seminal_reason: Established agriculture as a distinct economic sector with development implications, laying groundwork for analyzing production systems comparatively

Agrarian Scale And Farm Size Economics.abstract: Analysis of strategies to increase grain production in India, addressing scale, technology, and policy interventions in smallholder-dominated contexts

Agrarian Scale And Farm Size Economics.abstract: Addresses the definitional challenge of categorizing 'small-scale' versus 'large-scale' farmers in dual agricultural economies, establishing criteria for farm classification
Agrarian Scale And Farm Size Economics.seminal_reason: Confronted the fundamental methodological problem — how to define 'small' and 'large' — essential to all comparative research

Agrarian Scale And Farm Size Economics.abstract: Mapped global distribution of farm sizes and quantified smallholder contributions to food production, providing empirical foundation for debates on scale and productivity
Agrarian Scale And Farm Size Economics.seminal_reason: First global-scale empirical mapping of farm size distribution and smallholder production, establishing the empirical reality underlying policy debates

Agrarian Scale And Farm Size Economics.title: Are Small Farms Sustainable by Nature?—Review of an Ongoing Misunderstanding in Agroecology
Agrarian Scale And Farm Size Economics.abstract: Critically examined the assumption that small farms are inherently more sustainable, exposing normative bias in agroecology discourse
Agrarian Scale And Farm Size Economics.seminal_reason: Challenged foundational assumption linking scale to sustainability, forcing explicit examination of the mechanisms rather than scale itself

Agribusiness Systems And Industrial Inte.title: Agribusiness: A New Concept (implied from Davis and Goldberg, 1957, referenced in Q2/WebSearch)
Agribusiness Systems And Industrial Inte.authors_str: John H. Davis; Ray A. Goldberg
Agribusiness Systems And Industrial Inte.abstract: Coined the term 'agribusiness' and conceptualized agriculture as an integrated system of input supply, production, processing, and distribution rather than isolated farming
Agribusiness Systems And Industrial Inte.seminal_reason: Invented the intellectual category 'agribusiness' — the foundational concept defining agro-industrial integration

Agribusiness Systems And Industrial Inte.title: Globalisation, Localisation and Sustainable Livelihoods
Agribusiness Systems And Industrial Inte.abstract: Examined tension between globalization pressures and local livelihood sustainability, situating agricultural production systems within global economic transformations
Agribusiness Systems And Industrial Inte.seminal_reason: Framed agriculture within globalization debates, establishing the structural context shaping small-scale versus industrial production

Agricultural Mechanization And Technolog.title: Scaling agricultural mechanization services in smallholder farming systems: Case studies from sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia, and Latin America
Agricultural Mechanization And Technolog.abstract: Documented failed technology transfer and emergence of context-appropriate mechanization models for smallholders, challenging linear modernization narratives
Agricultural Mechanization And Technolog.seminal_reason: Established that mechanization is not scale-neutral, showing why 1980s-90s technology transfer failed and what alternatives emerged

Agricultural Mechanization And Technolog.title: Mechanization and sustainable agri-food system transformation in the Global South. A review
Agricultural Mechanization And Technolog.abstract: Synthesized evidence on mechanization's synergies and trade-offs with sustainability goals, addressing environmental, social, and economic dimensions in Global South contexts
Agricultural Mechanization And Technolog.seminal_reason: Synthesized mechanization's complex sustainability implications, moving beyond productivity to multi-dimensional assessment

Agricultural Typologies And Classificati.title: Typology and distribution of small farms in Europe: Towards a better picture
Agricultural Typologies And Classificati.abstract: Developed empirically-grounded typology of small farms in Europe, addressing the heterogeneity within the 'small farm' category and their diverse roles
Agricultural Typologies And Classificati.seminal_reason: Challenged monolithic treatment of 'small farms,' establishing typological sophistication in categorizing production units

Animal Intelligence And Boundary Problem.title: History of Animals
Animal Intelligence And Boundary Problem.abstract: Aristotle's systematic empirical investigation of animal life, documenting behavioral and cognitive capacities across species. Established comparative framework for understanding intelligence distribution in nature by observing animal memory, learning, and problem-solving.
Animal Intelligence And Boundary Problem.seminal_reason: First systematic comparative study of animal cognition, establishing empirical foundation for intelligence as scalar property across species
intersection_tags: Animal Intelligence x Hierarchy of Being

Animal Intelligence And Boundary Problem.title: Discourse on Method
Animal Intelligence And Boundary Problem.abstract: Descartes's methodological work presenting rational method and arguing animals lack reason and language, functioning as mere automata. Distinguished human rational soul as immaterial thinking substance from animal bodily mechanisms. Established language use as decisive criterion distinguishing human intelligence from animal behavior.
Animal Intelligence And Boundary Problem.seminal_reason: Made language the decisive mark of human intelligence absent in animals, establishing categorical human-animal cognitive divide in modern philosophy
intersection_tags: Animal Intelligence x Mechanical vs Organic Intelligence

Animal Intelligence And Boundary Problem.title: A Treatise of Human Nature
Animal Intelligence And Boundary Problem.abstract: Hume's radical empiricist epistemology and psychology arguing all knowledge derives from impressions and ideas connected by association. Dissolved substantial self into bundle of perceptions, reduced reason to slave of passions. Minimized human-animal cognitive difference, attributing reasoning and emotion to animals. Challenged rationalist conceptions of pure intellect.
Animal Intelligence And Boundary Problem.seminal_reason: Dissolved substantive human rationality into associationist psychology, radically narrowing gap between human and animal cognition
intersection_tags: Animal Intelligence x Innate vs Acquired Knowledge

Animal Intelligence And Boundary Problem.title: Critique of Practical Reason
Animal Intelligence And Boundary Problem.abstract: Kant's moral philosophy grounding ethics in autonomous practical reason. Distinguished human rational will capable of acting from duty and moral law from animal will determined by inclination. Positioned human intelligence as uniquely capable of self-legislation through reason, establishing dignity and moral worth absent in merely natural beings.
Animal Intelligence And Boundary Problem.seminal_reason: Made autonomous rational will defining feature of human intelligence, establishing moral dimension as human-specific cognitive capacity
intersection_tags: Animal Intelligence x Soul and Intellect

Artificial And Collective Intelligence.title: Computing Machinery and Intelligence
Artificial And Collective Intelligence.abstract: Proposes operational test (Turing Test) for machine intelligence based on conversational indistinguishability from human. Can machines think? Intelligence as computational function independent of biological substrate.
Artificial And Collective Intelligence.seminal_reason: Introduced artificial intelligence as possibility and functionalist criterion for intelligence — opened non-biological intelligence as philosophical category

Artificial And Collective Intelligence.title: The Structure of Scientific Revolutions
Artificial And Collective Intelligence.abstract: Paradigm shifts in scientific knowledge. Scientific intelligence as community-embedded, tradition-constituted practice rather than individual rational method. Incommensurability between paradigms.
Artificial And Collective Intelligence.seminal_reason: Challenged individualist rationalist model of intelligence — positioned understanding as socially embedded and historically contingent

Artificial And Collective Intelligence.title: Intentionality
Artificial And Collective Intelligence.abstract: Analysis of intentionality as intrinsic feature of mind. Intelligence requires original intentionality grounded in biological consciousness. Critique of computational theory — syntax insufficient for semantics.
Artificial And Collective Intelligence.seminal_reason: Challenged functionalist equivalence of biological and artificial intelligence — argued intentionality requires biological substrate

Artificial And Collective Intelligence.title: The Intentional Stance
Artificial And Collective Intelligence.abstract: Intelligence attributed through intentional stance — treating system as rational agent. Multiple levels: physical, design, intentional. Intelligence as pattern rather than substance, applicable to biological and artificial systems.
Artificial And Collective Intelligence.seminal_reason: Pragmatic reconception of intelligence as interpretive stance rather than intrinsic property — opened intelligence attribution to artifacts and collectives

Artificial And Collective Intelligence.title: The sociology of philosophies: a global theory of intellectual change
Artificial And Collective Intelligence.abstract: Intellectual networks and interaction rituals producing philosophical creativity across cultures. Intelligence as socially constituted through attention space and generational chains. Global comparative analysis of philosophical traditions.
Artificial And Collective Intelligence.seminal_reason: Sociological reconception of philosophical intelligence as network phenomenon rather than individual genius — comparative global scope

Artificial And Collective Intelligence.title: Philosophy, Mind, and Cognitive Inquiry
Artificial And Collective Intelligence.abstract: Interdisciplinary investigation of knowledge and information processing systems across human, animal, and machine domains. Addresses computational, biological, and epistemological dimensions of intelligence and cognition.
Artificial And Collective Intelligence.seminal_reason: Synthetic treatment of intelligence across biological and artificial substrates within unified information-processing framework

Artificial And Collective Intelligence.title: Multimodal Distributional Semantics
Artificial And Collective Intelligence.abstract: Computational models deriving semantic representations from co-occurrence patterns in text and perceptual data. Meaning as distributional pattern rather than referential correspondence. Integration of linguistic and perceptual information.
Artificial And Collective Intelligence.seminal_reason: Demonstrates intelligence as pattern recognition in multimodal data — challenges traditional symbolic and representational accounts of meaning

Atomism And Material Intelligence.title: Atomist fragments
Atomism And Material Intelligence.source: referenced in 'Democritean Conceptions in Brain Research' and 'Changing our minds: Democritus'
Atomism And Material Intelligence.abstract: Democritus co-founded atomic theory with Leucippus, proposing that mind and intelligence arise from configurations of fine, spherical atoms, and that the brain is the seat of thought—establishing early materialist account of intelligence
Atomism And Material Intelligence.seminal_reason: First systematic materialist account of intelligence as emergent from atomic configurations—established naturalistic alternative to transcendent theories of nous

Autonomy And Agency.title: Groundwork for the Metaphysics of Morals
Autonomy And Agency.abstract: Established autonomy as rational self-legislation according to universal moral law, defining human dignity as capacity to be moral authors of one's actions rather than mere means to external ends—foundational to deontological ethics and conceptions of human agency.
Autonomy And Agency.seminal_reason: Defined autonomy as rational self-legislation—normative foundation for evaluating AI's impact on human moral agency.
intersection_tags: Autonomy and Agency x Meaning and Human Flourishing

Categories Of Being.title: Categories
Categories Of Being.abstract: Aristotle's first systematic classification of being into ten categories (substance, quantity, quality, relation, place, time, position, state, action, affection)—providing conceptual framework for distinguishing entities and their properties
Categories Of Being.seminal_reason: First systematic ontological classification in Western philosophy—established conceptual architecture for categorizing all aspects of reality including mental capacities

Cognitive Development Self Concept.title: Identity: Youth and Crisis
Cognitive Development Self Concept.abstract: Erikson's influential work established identity formation as the central developmental task of adolescence. Identity achievement involves integrating past identifications, present capacities, and future possibilities into coherent self-understanding. This linked identity to psychological health and developmental progression.
Cognitive Development Self Concept.seminal_reason: Established identity formation as central developmental task and introduced the concept of identity crisis, showing that coherent self-understanding emerges through developmental stages and social-psychological synthesis

Cognitive Development Self Concept.title: The Self-Concept: Theory, Measurement, Development and Behaviour
Cognitive Development Self Concept.abstract: Burns provided comprehensive synthesis of self-concept research, integrating theory, measurement, and developmental perspectives. Established self-concept as multi-dimensional construct (academic self-concept, social self-concept, etc.) that develops through reflected appraisals and social comparison.
Cognitive Development Self Concept.seminal_reason: Synthesized self-concept research into systematic framework showing how self-understanding is multi-dimensional, develops across lifespan, and mediates between social experience and behavior

Consciousness And Intentionality.title: Intentionality
Consciousness And Intentionality.abstract: Searle's theory of intentionality as mind's directedness toward objects and states of affairs. Positioned intentionality as defining feature of mental states. Argued intentionality distinguished human (and possibly animal) minds from non-mental systems. Challenged computational theories of mind lacking intrinsic intentionality.
Consciousness And Intentionality.seminal_reason: Made intentionality the mark of genuine intelligence, challenging whether artificial systems possess real understanding
intersection_tags: Consciousness and Intentionality x Mechanical vs Organic Intelligence

Consciousness And Intentionality.title: The Intentional Stance
Consciousness And Intentionality.abstract: Dennett's pragmatic account of intentionality and intelligence. Argued intelligence attributable wherever intentional stance (interpreting system as rational agent with beliefs and desires) proves predictively useful. Dissolved sharp boundaries between human, animal, and artificial intelligence by making intelligence a matter of interpretive strategy rather than intrinsic property.
Consciousness And Intentionality.seminal_reason: Made intelligence instrumental interpretive framework rather than ontological category, blurring human-animal-machine boundaries
intersection_tags: Consciousness and Intentionality x Mechanical vs Organic Intelligence

Consciousness And Intentionality.title: The Conscious Mind: In Search of a Fundamental Theory
Consciousness And Intentionality.abstract: Chalmers's argument for the 'hard problem' of consciousness — explaining subjective phenomenal experience. Distinguished easy problems (cognitive functions) from hard problem (why experience accompanies cognition). Positioned phenomenal consciousness as irreducible to functional intelligence, potentially absent in artificial systems implementing same functions.
Consciousness And Intentionality.seminal_reason: Distinguished functional intelligence from phenomenal consciousness, questioning whether intelligence requires subjective experience
intersection_tags: Consciousness and Intentionality x Mechanical vs Organic Intelligence

Consciousness And Phenomenal Experience.title: Education for Critical Consciousness
Consciousness And Phenomenal Experience.abstract: Argued that true education develops critical consciousness (conscientização) enabling learners to perceive social, political, and economic contradictions and take action against oppressive elements—established consciousness as inherently social, dialogical, and political rather than individual cognitive property.
Consciousness And Phenomenal Experience.seminal_reason: Established consciousness as social and political praxis—challenges individualist/computational models underlying AI consciousness claims.

Consciousness And Phenomenal Experience.title: Black Feminist Thought: Knowledge, Consciousness, and the Politics of Empowerment
Consciousness And Phenomenal Experience.abstract: Argued that Black women's experiences produce distinctive standpoint yielding unique knowledge claims and critical consciousness about intersecting oppressions—challenged dominant epistemologies and established that knowledge and consciousness are socially situated rather than universal abstractions.
Consciousness And Phenomenal Experience.seminal_reason: Established knowledge as socially situated and embodied—challenges universal/computational models of intelligence and consciousness.

Consciousness And Phenomenal Experience.title: The Conscious Mind: In Search of a Fundamental Theory
Consciousness And Phenomenal Experience.abstract: Distinguished 'easy problems' of consciousness (functional mechanisms) from the 'hard problem' (why physical processes give rise to subjective experience), explicitly examining strong AI hypothesis and arguing that computational functionalism cannot account for phenomenal consciousness—foundational to consciousness studies.
Consciousness And Phenomenal Experience.seminal_reason: Crystallized hard problem of consciousness—established that functional AI explanations cannot address phenomenal experience.
intersection_tags: Consciousness x Intentionality and Mental States

Consciousness And Phenomenal Experience.title: The Feeling of What Happens: Body and Emotion in the Making of Consciousness
Consciousness And Phenomenal Experience.abstract: Neuroscientific account arguing that consciousness emerges from body-brain interactions, with emotions and feelings serving as foundation for selfhood—challenged disembodied computational models and established embodiment as necessary for consciousness.
Consciousness And Phenomenal Experience.seminal_reason: Established embodiment as necessary for consciousness—challenges disembodied AI's capacity for genuine subjective experience.

Consciousness And Phenomenal Experience.title: The Blackwell Guide to Philosophy of Mind
Consciousness And Phenomenal Experience.abstract: Comprehensive collection of essays surveying central topics in philosophy of mind including consciousness, intentionality, mental causation, and artificial intelligence—synthesized diverse perspectives on mind-body problem and computational theory of mind.
Consciousness And Phenomenal Experience.seminal_reason: Authoritative survey integrating diverse philosophy of mind traditions—comprehensive foundation for AI consciousness debates.
intersection_tags: Consciousness x Intentionality and Mental States

Consciousness And Phenomenal Experience.title: Other Minds: The Octopus, the Sea, and the Deep Origins of Consciousness
Consciousness And Phenomenal Experience.abstract: Explored consciousness in cephalopods as radically different evolutionary pathway to intelligence, demonstrating that minds can emerge through diverse architectures—challenges anthropocentric assumptions about intelligence and consciousness relevant to evaluating non-human (including artificial) minds.
Consciousness And Phenomenal Experience.seminal_reason: Demonstrated diverse evolutionary paths to intelligence—challenges anthropocentric criteria for evaluating machine minds.
intersection_tags: Consciousness x Human vs. Animal Cognition

Consciousness And Self-Awareness.title: The Vocabulary of Philosophy, Mental, Moral, and Metaphysical
Consciousness And Self-Awareness.abstract: Mid-19th century comprehensive philosophical lexicon defining core concepts in philosophy of mind, morality, and metaphysics with historical quotations and references
Consciousness And Self-Awareness.seminal_reason: Documented how philosophical concepts of mind and identity were understood in mid-19th century, pre-dating modern psychology
intersection_tags: Consciousness and Self-Awareness x Personal Identity and Continuity

Consciousness And Self-Awareness.title: A Cognitive Theory of Consciousness
Consciousness And Self-Awareness.abstract: Baars's global workspace theory proposing consciousness as a cognitive mechanism for integrating and broadcasting information across neural systems, establishing cognitive science foundations for consciousness studies
Consciousness And Self-Awareness.seminal_reason: Established global workspace theory linking consciousness to cognitive architecture
intersection_tags: Consciousness and Self-Awareness x Intelligence Measurement and Psychometrics

Consciousness And Self-Awareness.title: The Conscious Mind: In Search of a Fundamental Theory
Consciousness And Self-Awareness.abstract: Chalmers's influential work distinguishing the 'hard problem' of phenomenal consciousness from easier problems of cognitive function, establishing foundations for consciousness studies and the explanatory gap problem
Consciousness And Self-Awareness.seminal_reason: Established the hard problem of consciousness as central philosophical challenge
intersection_tags: Consciousness and Self-Awareness x Mind-Brain Identity Theory

Consciousness And Self-Awareness.title: The Feeling of What Happens: Body and Emotion in the Making of Consciousness
Consciousness And Self-Awareness.abstract: Damasio's neuroscientific account of consciousness as grounded in bodily awareness and emotion, establishing embodied foundations for self-construction and conscious experience
Consciousness And Self-Awareness.seminal_reason: Established embodied, emotion-based neuroscientific theory of consciousness and self
intersection_tags: Consciousness and Self-Awareness x Personal Identity and Continuity

Consciousness And Self-Awareness.title: Encyclopedia of Consciousness
Consciousness And Self-Awareness.abstract: Comprehensive reference covering consciousness from multiple disciplinary perspectives including philosophy, neuroscience, psychology, and cognitive science
Consciousness And Self-Awareness.seminal_reason: Encyclopedic synthesis of consciousness studies across disciplines
intersection_tags: Consciousness and Self-Awareness x Mind-Brain Identity Theory

Consciousness Mind Body Problem.title: Meditations on First Philosophy
Consciousness Mind Body Problem.abstract: Descartes's substance dualism established the mind-body problem in its modern form—arguing that mind (res cogitans) and body (res extensa) are fundamentally different substances. The 'cogito' ('I think therefore I am') grounded identity in thinking substance, establishing consciousness as foundation of self-knowledge.
Consciousness Mind Body Problem.seminal_reason: Established modern mind-body dualism and grounded personal identity in thinking substance (res cogitans), founding the philosophical tradition treating consciousness as metaphysically distinct from physical processes

Consciousness Mind Body Problem.title: Philosophy of Mind
Consciousness Mind Body Problem.abstract: Kim's comprehensive textbook synthesizes contemporary philosophy of mind, including the mind-body problem, mental causation, functionalism, and consciousness. It provides systematic treatment of how mental states (including cognitive capacities like intelligence) relate to physical states and whether mental properties are reducible.
Consciousness Mind Body Problem.seminal_reason: Synthesized contemporary philosophy of mind including mental causation, functionalism, and the mind-body problem, providing systematic framework for understanding how mental capacities relate to physical substrate

Critical Consciousness And Liberation.title: Education for Critical Consciousness
Critical Consciousness And Liberation.abstract: Freire's pedagogical framework establishing that oppressed people can develop critical consciousness (conscientização) of their social conditions through dialogue and reflection, linking social identity position to particular forms of cognitive liberation
Critical Consciousness And Liberation.seminal_reason: Established critical consciousness as identity-linked cognitive development for oppressed groups
intersection_tags: Critical Consciousness and Liberation x Social Identity and Group Membership
Critical Consciousness and Liberation x Cognitive Development Theory

Cultural Contextual Intelligence.title: The Structure of Scientific Revolutions
Cultural Contextual Intelligence.abstract: While primarily philosophy of science, Kuhn established that knowledge is paradigm-dependent and socially constructed. This influenced social constructionist views of identity and intelligence, showing how 'facts' depend on conceptual frameworks and community practices.
Cultural Contextual Intelligence.seminal_reason: Established that scientific knowledge is paradigm-dependent and socially constructed, influencing later critiques that intelligence and identity are not natural kinds but conceptual frameworks shaped by social practices

Dianoia And Discursive Reasoning.title: Prior Analytics
Dianoia And Discursive Reasoning.source: referenced in 'Division, Syllogistic, and Science in Prior Analytics I.31'
Dianoia And Discursive Reasoning.abstract: Aristotle's treatise establishing the syllogism as formal logical system—first systematic account of deductive reasoning as distinct from Platonic division, foundational for understanding logical intelligence
Dianoia And Discursive Reasoning.seminal_reason: Invented formal logic and the syllogism—established deductive reasoning as systematic method distinct from division, foundational for all subsequent theories of rational intelligence

Dianoia And Discursive Reasoning.title: Commentary on Aristotle's De Anima
Dianoia And Discursive Reasoning.source: referenced in 'The Soul and Discursive Reason in the Philosophy of Proclus'
Dianoia And Discursive Reasoning.abstract: Proclus' Neoplatonic interpretation of Aristotle's psychology emphasizing dianoia as soul's discursive activity making itself into divided image of unified Nous—systematic theory of thought's logoi as divisions of prior unity
Dianoia And Discursive Reasoning.seminal_reason: Developed systematic Neoplatonic theory of dianoia as soul's self-division into logoi—established discursive reasoning as intermediate between unified divine nous and multiplicity of sensible world

Divine Intelligence And Cosmic Mind.title: Symposium
Divine Intelligence And Cosmic Mind.source: implied in discussions of daemonic and Diotima
Divine Intelligence And Cosmic Mind.abstract: Plato's dialogue on love (eros) including Diotima's teaching that the daemonic mediates between mortal and divine, and that eros drives ascent from physical beauty to knowledge of the Form of Beauty
Divine Intelligence And Cosmic Mind.seminal_reason: Established the daemonic (daimonion) as intermediary between human and divine intelligence—foundational for understanding human cognition as participating in but distinct from divine nous

Divine Intelligence And Cosmic Mind.title: Timaeus
Divine Intelligence And Cosmic Mind.source: implied in discussions of cosmic intelligence and demiurge
Divine Intelligence And Cosmic Mind.abstract: Plato's cosmological dialogue presenting the Demiurge as divine craftsman who orders the cosmos according to eternal Forms—establishing cosmic intelligence as mediator between eternal forms and material world
Divine Intelligence And Cosmic Mind.seminal_reason: Introduced cosmic intelligence (Demiurge) as ordering principle of nature—foundational for teleological views of intelligence in nature

Divine Intelligence And Cosmic Mind.title: Metaphysics
Divine Intelligence And Cosmic Mind.source: implied in sources discussing hylomorphism and divine intellect
Divine Intelligence And Cosmic Mind.abstract: Aristotle's first philosophy examining being qua being, introducing the unmoved mover as pure actuality and thought thinking itself—establishing divine intelligence as the highest form of nous
Divine Intelligence And Cosmic Mind.seminal_reason: Introduced the concept of divine intellect (nous theios) as pure thought thinking itself—established the apex of intelligence hierarchy against which all other forms are measured

Divine Intelligence And Cosmic Mind.title: Enneads
Divine Intelligence And Cosmic Mind.source: referenced in discussions of self-intellection and Neoplatonism
Divine Intelligence And Cosmic Mind.abstract: Plotinus' treatises developing Neoplatonic metaphysics with the One, Nous, and Soul as hypostases—establishing nous as divine intellect containing all Forms and human soul as participating in but distinct from divine intelligence
Divine Intelligence And Cosmic Mind.seminal_reason: Systematized Neoplatonic hierarchy with Nous as second hypostasis containing all Forms—established influential model of intelligence as emanation from transcendent unity

Divine Vs Human Intelligence.title: Republic
Divine Vs Human Intelligence.abstract: Plato's masterwork on justice and the ideal state, containing the Allegory of the Cave and theory of Forms. Establishes knowledge (episteme) as intellectual grasp of eternal Forms versus opinion (doxa) about sensible particulars. Positions philosopher-king as possessing highest human intelligence approaching divine wisdom.
Divine Vs Human Intelligence.seminal_reason: Distinguished knowledge from opinion and established intellect's ascent toward Forms as path from human to divine cognition
intersection_tags: Divine vs Human Intelligence x Innate vs Acquired Knowledge

Divine Vs Human Intelligence.title: Timaeus
Divine Vs Human Intelligence.abstract: Plato's cosmological dialogue describing the Demiurge's creation of the world-soul and individual souls. Presents tripartite psychology distinguishing rational (logistikon), spirited (thymoeides), and appetitive (epithymetikon) parts of soul. Positions human rational soul as participated copy of divine intelligence.
Divine Vs Human Intelligence.seminal_reason: Established Platonic psychology with reason as divine spark in humans, creating foundational framework for human intelligence as participation in transcendent Nous
intersection_tags: Divine vs Human Intelligence x Soul and Intellect

Divine Vs Human Intelligence.title: Confessions
Divine Vs Human Intelligence.abstract: Augustine's autobiographical and theological work exploring memory, time, and the soul's ascent to God through reason illuminated by divine grace. Synthesized Platonic philosophy with Christian theology, positioning human intelligence as created in God's image but damaged by sin, requiring divine illumination for true knowledge.
Divine Vs Human Intelligence.seminal_reason: Christianized Platonic epistemology by making divine illumination necessary for human intellectual grasp of eternal truths
intersection_tags: Divine vs Human Intelligence x Innate vs Acquired Knowledge

Divine Vs Human Intelligence.title: De Trinitate (On the Trinity)
Divine Vs Human Intelligence.abstract: Augustine's extended theological and philosophical investigation of the Trinity, using psychological analogies from human mind to understand divine nature. Established triadic structure of human intellect (memory-understanding-will) as image of God, positioning human intelligence as analogical participation in divine Trinity.
Divine Vs Human Intelligence.seminal_reason: Established human mind's triadic structure as imago Dei, making human intelligence definitionally theomorphic
intersection_tags: Divine vs Human Intelligence x Soul and Intellect

Divine Vs Human Intelligence.title: The Consolation of Philosophy
Divine Vs Human Intelligence.abstract: Boethius's philosophical work written in prison before execution, blending Platonic, Aristotelian, and Stoic philosophy with Christian theology. Distinguished human rational understanding from divine providence, positioning human intelligence as temporal and discursive versus God's eternal and simultaneous knowledge.
Divine Vs Human Intelligence.seminal_reason: Distinguished discursive human reason from God's eternal simultaneous knowledge, establishing temporal limitation as defining feature of human intelligence
intersection_tags: Divine vs Human Intelligence x Soul and Intellect

Divine Vs Human Intelligence.title: Critique of Pure Reason
Divine Vs Human Intelligence.abstract: Kant's critical philosophy synthesizing rationalism and empiricism. Argued human cognition requires both sensory intuition and conceptual understanding structured by a priori categories. Distinguished phenomena (appearances structured by human cognitive forms) from noumena (things-in-themselves). Positioned human intelligence as finite discursive understanding requiring sensible input, contrasted with hypothetical divine intellectual intuition that creates its objects.
Divine Vs Human Intelligence.seminal_reason: Established human intelligence as finite discursive understanding requiring sensory input, fundamentally distinct from divine intellectual intuition
intersection_tags: Divine vs Human Intelligence x Innate vs Acquired Knowledge

Divine Vs Human Intelligence.title: Phenomenology of Spirit
Divine Vs Human Intelligence.abstract: Hegel's account of consciousness's dialectical development from sense-certainty through self-consciousness to absolute knowing. Positioned human intelligence as moment in Geist's (Spirit's) self-realization, overcoming Kantian separation of subject and object. Human mind not static faculty but historically developing, achieving self-consciousness through social recognition and cultural formation.
Divine Vs Human Intelligence.seminal_reason: Historicized intelligence as dialectically developing Spirit, dissolving fixed categories into developmental process
intersection_tags: Divine vs Human Intelligence x Soul and Intellect

Ecosystem Services And Environmental Ext.title: EFFECTS OF BIODIVERSITY ON ECOSYSTEM FUNCTIONING: A CONSENSUS OF CURRENT KNOWLEDGE
Ecosystem Services And Environmental Ext.authors_str: David U. Hooper; F. Stuart Chapin
Ecosystem Services And Environmental Ext.abstract: Synthesized evidence linking biodiversity to ecosystem functioning, establishing that biological diversity affects productivity, stability, and resilience — relevant to agricultural ecosystem services
Ecosystem Services And Environmental Ext.seminal_reason: Established biodiversity as a measurable ecosystem function, foundational to environmental performance assessment in agriculture

Embodied And Extended Mind.title: The Feeling of What Happens: Body and Emotion in the Making of Consciousness
Embodied And Extended Mind.abstract: Damasio's neuroscientific account of consciousness grounded in bodily emotion and feeling. Argued consciousness emerges from organism's sense of embodied self. Positioned intelligence as inseparable from emotional and somatic processes. Challenged disembodied rationalist models of mind.
Embodied And Extended Mind.seminal_reason: Grounded consciousness and intelligence in embodied emotion, challenging Cartesian separation of reason from body and feeling
intersection_tags: Embodied and Extended Mind x Consciousness and Intentionality

Embodied Situated Cognition.title: Being and Time
Embodied Situated Cognition.abstract: Phenomenological analysis of human existence (Dasein) as Being-in-the-world. Intelligence not detached contemplation but engaged practical coping. Understanding as pre-theoretical disclosure of world rather than mental representation.
Embodied Situated Cognition.seminal_reason: Revolutionary reconception of intelligence as embedded practical engagement rather than detached cognition — challenged Cartesian subject-object framework

Embodied Situated Cognition.title: Phenomenology of Perception
Embodied Situated Cognition.abstract: Intelligence as embodied and perceptually grounded. Body-subject as condition of intentionality and understanding. Critique of intellectualism — intelligence inseparable from sensory-motor skills and lived body.
Embodied Situated Cognition.seminal_reason: Established embodiment as constitutive of intelligence rather than mere instrument of mind — foundational for embodied cognition paradigm

Embodiment And Situatedness.title: Being and Time
Embodiment And Situatedness.abstract: Phenomenological investigation of human existence (Dasein) as fundamentally characterized by being-in-the-world, establishing that humans are not detached subjects observing objects but beings always already embedded in meaningful contexts—foundational to embodied, situated cognition critique of computational mind.
Embodiment And Situatedness.seminal_reason: Established human existence as fundamentally situated and world-embedded—philosophical foundation for embodied cognition critiques of AI.
intersection_tags: Embodiment and Situatedness x Tool-User Ontology

Embodiment And Situatedness.title: The Fate of Place: A Philosophical History
Embodiment And Situatedness.abstract: Comprehensive philosophical history tracing Western philosophy's progressive neglect of place from ancient Greece through modernity, arguing for place as irreducible to space and fundamental to human existence—established philosophical foundations for understanding 'place' as situated, lived location rather than abstract position.
Embodiment And Situatedness.seminal_reason: Definitive philosophical history of 'place'—establishes 'place' as existential situatedness beyond functional role.
intersection_tags: Embodiment and Situatedness x Tool-User Ontology

Essentialism Categorization.title: The Essential Child
Essentialism Categorization.abstract: Gelman demonstrated that essentialism — the belief that categories like 'dog,' 'man,' or 'intelligence' have underlying true natures — is an early cognitive bias in children, establishing that essentialist thinking about identity and capacity is developmentally foundational rather than culturally learned
Essentialism Categorization.seminal_reason: Demonstrated psychological essentialism as early cognitive bias — established that essentialist thinking about identity and intelligence is developmentally primitive

Evolutionary Continuity.title: On the Origin of Species
Evolutionary Continuity.abstract: Darwin's theory of evolution by natural selection, establishing common descent of all life including humans. Positioned human intelligence as product of gradual evolutionary development from animal ancestors, challenging categorical human exceptionalism. Implied mental faculties differ in degree rather than kind across species.
Evolutionary Continuity.seminal_reason: Placed human intelligence on evolutionary continuum with animal cognition, undermining metaphysical human-animal divide
intersection_tags: Hierarchy of Being x Evolutionary Continuity

Evolutionary Continuity.title: The Descent of Man
Evolutionary Continuity.abstract: Darwin's explicit application of evolutionary theory to human origins, arguing human mental and moral faculties evolved from animal precursors. Documented cognitive abilities in animals (memory, attention, reasoning, tool use) differing from humans in degree not kind. Dissolved traditional human-animal boundary in naturalistic framework.
Evolutionary Continuity.seminal_reason: Explicitly argued human intelligence evolved from animal cognition by degrees, naturalizing mind within biological continuity

Evolutionary Continuity.title: Beyond Good and Evil
Evolutionary Continuity.abstract: Nietzsche's critique of traditional philosophy and morality, questioning metaphysical truth-claims and rational foundations. Argued reason itself serves life-preserving and power-enhancing drives. Challenged human intellectual exceptionalism, viewing intelligence as refined animal instinct. Positioned truth-seeking as one perspectival interpretation among others.
Evolutionary Continuity.seminal_reason: Naturalized reason as refined instinct serving will to power, undermining metaphysical status of human intelligence
intersection_tags: Evolutionary Continuity x Soul and Intellect

Evolutionary Continuity.title: On the Genealogy of Morals
Evolutionary Continuity.abstract: Nietzsche's genealogical analysis of moral concepts, revealing their historical origins in power relations rather than rational insight. Human intelligence characterized by capacity for memory, promising, and bad conscience — products of social discipline. Intelligence emerges through violent self-constraint, making humans 'sick animal.'
Evolutionary Continuity.seminal_reason: Presented human rational consciousness as pathological product of social repression, not natural excellence
intersection_tags: Evolutionary Continuity x Soul and Intellect

Evolutionary Naturalization.title: On the Origin of Species
Evolutionary Naturalization.abstract: Established continuity of all life through natural selection and common descent. Mental faculties including intelligence subject to gradual evolution. No absolute distinction between human and animal intelligence, only degree.
Evolutionary Naturalization.seminal_reason: Revolutionary naturalization of intelligence as biological adaptation with continuous gradation across species — shattered metaphysical human-animal divide

Evolutionary Naturalization.title: The Descent of Man
Evolutionary Naturalization.abstract: Explicit treatment of human intelligence evolution. Argues mental powers including reason, language, and moral sense evolved gradually from animal ancestors. Differences between human and animal intelligence are of degree not kind.
Evolutionary Naturalization.seminal_reason: Direct challenge to human exceptionalism in intelligence — positioned human cognition as continuous with animal faculties through evolutionary mechanisms

Evolutionary Naturalization.title: The Expression of the Emotions in Man and Animals
Evolutionary Naturalization.abstract: Comparative study of emotional expression across species. Establishes evolutionary continuity in affective and cognitive capacities. Intelligence inseparable from emotional and bodily systems.
Evolutionary Naturalization.seminal_reason: Extended evolutionary continuity to affective and expressive dimensions of intelligence, challenging rationalist separation of reason from emotion and body

Evolutionary Naturalization.title: The Principles of Psychology
Evolutionary Naturalization.abstract: Comprehensive psychological treatise treating intelligence as adaptive biological function. Stream of consciousness, habit, instinct, and reasoning as integrated aspects of mental life. Intelligence as practical problem-solving capacity shaped by evolutionary pressures.
Evolutionary Naturalization.seminal_reason: Established psychology as empirical science of intelligence and positioned cognitive capacities as embodied, adaptive functions rather than pure rational faculties

Evolutionary Naturalization.title: Animal Intelligence
Evolutionary Naturalization.abstract: Experimental study of animal learning through puzzle boxes. Established trial-and-error learning and law of effect. Intelligence as behavioral adaptation measurable across species through standardized tasks.
Evolutionary Naturalization.seminal_reason: Founded comparative psychology and established experimental methods for measuring intelligence across species — operationalized intelligence as problem-solving behavior

Evolutionary Naturalization.title: The Growth of Biological Thought: Diversity, Evolution, and Inheritance
Evolutionary Naturalization.abstract: Comprehensive history of biological concepts including evolution, systematics, and development. Examines conceptual transformations in understanding life, adaptation, and mental capacities within evolutionary framework.
Evolutionary Naturalization.seminal_reason: Synthesized evolutionary biology's conceptual history including naturalization of intelligence and mental capacities within population thinking and selection

Evolutionary Naturalization.title: Using Neuroscience to Help Understand Fear and Anxiety: A Two-System Framework
Evolutionary Naturalization.authors_str: Joseph E. LeDoux; Daniel S. Pine
Evolutionary Naturalization.abstract: Neural systems underlying threat detection and response. Dissociates defensive survival circuits (shared across species) from conscious fear experiences (possibly human-specific). Brain-behavior relationships in emotional intelligence.
Evolutionary Naturalization.seminal_reason: Neuroscientific grounding of emotional intelligence and species continuity in survival circuits — challenges purely rational models of intelligence

Evolutionary Naturalization.title: A critique of pure learning and what artificial neural networks can learn from animal brains
Evolutionary Naturalization.abstract: Contrasts data-hungry artificial neural network training with efficient animal learning. Animals leverage evolved genomic priors — intelligence as interaction between innate structure and learning. Critique of pure empiricism in AI.
Evolutionary Naturalization.seminal_reason: Demonstrates evolutionary intelligence as genomically encoded priors enabling rapid learning — challenges blank slate empiricism in both biological and artificial intelligence

Greek Nous And Dianoia.title: The Republic
Greek Nous And Dianoia.abstract: Plato's foundational dialogue establishing the divided line metaphor distinguishing discursive reason (dianoia) from intuitive intellect (nous), and positioning philosophical intelligence as capacity to grasp eternal Forms beyond sensory appearance.
Greek Nous And Dianoia.seminal_reason: First systematic Western distinction between types of cognitive capacity based on objects known — established intelligence as grasp of immaterial universals versus material particulars

Greek Nous And Dianoia.title: Nicomachean Ethics
Greek Nous And Dianoia.abstract: Distinguishes types of intellectual virtue: theoretical wisdom (sophia), practical wisdom (phronesis), and technical skill (techne). Establishes contemplative intelligence (theoria) as highest human activity and closest approximation to divine thought.
Greek Nous And Dianoia.seminal_reason: First systematic categorization of intelligence types by function and object — theoretical versus practical reason distinction foundational to Western philosophy

Greek Nous And Dianoia.title: The Enneads
Greek Nous And Dianoia.abstract: Neoplatonic elaboration of intelligence (Nous) as second hypostasis emanating from the One. Intelligence as eternal realm of Forms, superior to human discursive reasoning which operates in time. Human intelligence participates in but does not equal divine Intellect.
Greek Nous And Dianoia.seminal_reason: Established metaphysical hierarchy of intelligence levels from divine Nous to human reasoning, framework adopted by Christian, Islamic, and Jewish medieval philosophy

Hierarchy Of Being (Great Chain).title: The Guide for the Perplexed
Hierarchy Of Being (Great Chain).abstract: Maimonides's masterwork reconciling Aristotelian philosophy with Jewish theology. Distinguished levels of intellection from material to agent intellect, positioning human rational perfection as conjunction with Active Intellect (identified with lowest angelic intelligence). Established hierarchical chain from human reason through angelic intelligences to God.
Hierarchy Of Being (Great Chain).seminal_reason: Systematized medieval Jewish philosophy's hierarchy of intellects from human through angelic to divine, making human intelligence lowest rung on ladder of immaterial minds
intersection_tags: Hierarchy of Being x Active vs Passive Intellect

Hierarchy Of Being (Great Chain).title: Summa contra Gentiles
Hierarchy Of Being (Great Chain).abstract: Aquinas's philosophical defense of Christian theology written to aid Dominican missionaries. Systematically distinguished grades of being and knowledge: God as pure intellect, angels as pure forms knowing intuitively, humans as rational animals knowing discursively from sensory abstraction, animals as sensitive souls without reason. Established human as boundary being — lowest intellectual substance and highest animal.
Hierarchy Of Being (Great Chain).seminal_reason: Positioned human as ontological middle being — intellectual substance requiring body, bridging material and immaterial realms
intersection_tags: Hierarchy of Being x Mind-Body Problem
Animal Intelligence x Soul and Intellect

Hierarchy Of Being (Great Chain).title: Monadology
Hierarchy Of Being (Great Chain).abstract: Leibniz's mature metaphysical system presenting universe as composed of monads — simple immaterial substances with perception and appetition. Distinguished bare monads (matter), souls (animals with memory), and spirits/minds (humans with reason and self-consciousness). Positioned human intelligence as highest created monad below God, with innate ideas unfolding through experience. Rejected Cartesian mechanism, giving animals genuine psychic life.
Hierarchy Of Being (Great Chain).seminal_reason: Established graduated hierarchy of psychic substances from matter through animal soul to human spirit, restoring continuity while maintaining human rational exceptionalism
intersection_tags: Hierarchy of Being x Animal Intelligence
Innate vs Acquired Knowledge x Soul and Intellect

Hierarchy Of Being (Great Chain).title: The World as Will and Representation
Hierarchy Of Being (Great Chain).abstract: Schopenhauer's metaphysics positing blind Will as thing-in-itself underlying phenomenal representations. Positioned intellect as Will's tool for survival, secondary to unconscious drive. Distinguished grades of Will's objectification from inorganic matter through plant and animal life to human consciousness. Human intelligence enables temporary escape from Will through aesthetic contemplation and ascetic denial.
Hierarchy Of Being (Great Chain).seminal_reason: Subordinated intellect to unconscious Will, making intelligence instrumental organ rather than essential human nature
intersection_tags: Hierarchy of Being x Soul and Intellect

Hierarchy Of Being (Great Chain).title: Cosmos: A Sketch of a Physical Description of the Universe
Hierarchy Of Being (Great Chain).abstract: Humboldt's synthetic work unifying physical, biological, and human dimensions of nature. Presented cosmos as interconnected organic whole with humans as part of, not separate from, natural order. Positioned human intelligence within total natural system, anticipating ecological perspective.
Hierarchy Of Being (Great Chain).seminal_reason: Positioned human intelligence within total cosmic system, pioneering holistic naturalism integrating human mind into physical nature
intersection_tags: Hierarchy of Being x Evolutionary Continuity

Human Vs. Animal Cognition.title: The Origin of Species
Human Vs. Animal Cognition.abstract: Established evolutionary continuity between human and animal intelligence, placing humans on a cognitive continuum rather than as categorically distinct—foundational for understanding intelligence as gradient phenomenon rather than binary property.
Human Vs. Animal Cognition.seminal_reason: Dissolved human/animal intelligence binary, establishing intelligence as spectrum—crucial for evaluating machine intelligence claims.
intersection_tags: Human vs. Animal Cognition x Multiple Forms of Intelligence

Human Vs. Animal Cognition.title: Animal Intelligence: An Experimental Study of the Associative Processes in Animals
Human Vs. Animal Cognition.abstract: Brought animal behavior into laboratory for objective scrutiny through puzzle box experiments, concluding that apparent animal intelligence resulted from trial-and-error learning rather than reasoning—early mechanistic account of intelligence as stimulus-response patterns.
Human Vs. Animal Cognition.seminal_reason: First experimental reduction of intelligence to mechanistic learning—precursor to behaviorist and computational models.
intersection_tags: Human vs. Animal Cognition x Intelligence as Abstraction

Innate Vs Acquired Knowledge.title: An Essay Concerning Human Understanding
Innate Vs Acquired Knowledge.abstract: Locke's foundational empiricist work rejecting innate ideas and arguing all knowledge derives from sensory experience. Presented mind as tabula rasa (blank slate) receiving simple ideas from sensation and reflection, combining them into complex ideas. Positioned human intelligence as active power organizing experience, distinct from divine omniscience and animal sensation by capacity for abstract reasoning.
Innate Vs Acquired Knowledge.seminal_reason: Established empiricist epistemology rejecting innatism, making human intelligence product of experiential learning rather than divine pre-programming
intersection_tags: Innate vs Acquired Knowledge x Soul and Intellect

Intellectual Virtues.title: Theaetetus
Intellectual Virtues.abstract: Plato's dialogue examining the nature of knowledge (episteme) and its relation to perception and true belief—foundational for epistemology and the question of what distinguishes knowledge from mere opinion
Intellectual Virtues.seminal_reason: First sustained philosophical investigation of the nature of knowledge (episteme) as distinct from perception and belief—established epistemology as central to understanding intelligence
categories: Sophia And Phronesis As Wisdom Forms
theme_tags: Sophia and phronesis as wisdom forms

Intellectual Virtues.title: Nicomachean Ethics
Intellectual Virtues.abstract: Aristotle's systematic treatise on ethics, Book VI of which distinguishes five intellectual virtues (episteme, techne, nous, sophia, phronesis) representing the first sustained taxonomy of intelligence types in Western philosophy
Intellectual Virtues.seminal_reason: First systematic classification of intellectual virtues and modes of knowledge—foundational distinction between theoretical and practical intelligence
categories: Sophia And Phronesis As Wisdom Forms
theme_tags: Sophia and phronesis as wisdom forms

Intellectual Virtues.title: Posterior Analytics
Intellectual Virtues.source: implied in discussions of episteme and demonstration
Intellectual Virtues.abstract: Aristotle's treatise on scientific knowledge (episteme) as demonstration from first principles apprehended by nous—establishing the structure of demonstrative science and the role of intuitive intellect
Intellectual Virtues.seminal_reason: Established demonstrative science (episteme apodeiktike) and the role of nous in grasping first principles—foundational for understanding scientific intelligence as distinct from empirical observation

Intelligence As Abstraction And Problem-.title: Tao Te Ching
Intelligence As Abstraction And Problem-.abstract: Ancient Chinese philosophical text addressing the nature of wisdom, intelligence, and the relationship between action and non-action (wu wei), establishing foundational Eastern conceptions of intelligence as harmony with natural patterns rather than computational abstraction.
Intelligence As Abstraction And Problem-.seminal_reason: Established non-Western conception of intelligence as attunement rather than problem-solving—alternative foundation for evaluating AI.
intersection_tags: Intelligence as Abstraction x Embodiment and Situatedness

Intelligence As Abstraction And Problem-.title: General Intelligence, Objectively Determined and Measured
Intelligence As Abstraction And Problem-.abstract: Introduced the concept of general intelligence (g-factor) through factor analysis of cognitive test correlations, establishing psychometric tradition of intelligence as single underlying capacity—most influential paper in history of psychometric intelligence theory.
Intelligence As Abstraction And Problem-.seminal_reason: Established g-factor theory—foundational to psychometric intelligence measurement and debates about AI general vs. narrow intelligence.
intersection_tags: Intelligence as Abstraction x Multiple Forms of Intelligence

Intelligence As Abstraction And Problem-.title: Computing Machinery and Intelligence
Intelligence As Abstraction And Problem-.abstract: Proposed the Turing Test as an operational criterion for machine intelligence, reframing the question 'Can machines think?' as 'Can machines exhibit intelligent behavior indistinguishable from humans?'—foundational to AI philosophy by shifting from essence to function.
Intelligence As Abstraction And Problem-.seminal_reason: Established behavioral/functional test for machine intelligence—foundational shift from metaphysics to pragmatics in AI.
intersection_tags: Intelligence as Abstraction x Intentionality and Mental States

Intelligence As Abstraction And Problem-.title: Intelligence: Knowns and Unknowns
authors: American Psychological Association Board of Scientific Affairs
Intelligence As Abstraction And Problem-.authors_str: American Psychological Association Board of Scientific Affairs
Intelligence As Abstraction And Problem-.abstract: Consensus report defining intelligence as capacity for abstraction, logic, understanding, self-awareness, learning, emotional knowledge, reasoning, planning, creativity, critical thinking, and problem-solving—established comprehensive multidimensional definition synthesizing diverse psychological traditions.
Intelligence As Abstraction And Problem-.seminal_reason: Authoritative consensus definition integrating diverse traditions—benchmark for evaluating AI capabilities against human intelligence.
intersection_tags: Intelligence as Abstraction x Multiple Forms of Intelligence

Intelligence As Abstraction And Problem-.title: Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach
Intelligence As Abstraction And Problem-.abstract: Definitive textbook establishing AI as the study of rational agents that perceive environments and take actions maximizing expected utility—unified diverse AI approaches under agent-based framework and became standard introduction to field across four editions.
Intelligence As Abstraction And Problem-.seminal_reason: Canonical textbook defining modern AI paradigm—established rational agent framework dominating contemporary AI research.
intersection_tags: Intelligence as Abstraction x Prediction and Control

Intelligence As Concept.title: Metaphors of Mind
Intelligence As Concept.abstract: Examines metaphorical frameworks shaping theories of intelligence: geographic, computational, biological, epistemological, anthropological, sociological. How metaphors constrain and enable understanding of mind across disciplines.
Intelligence As Concept.seminal_reason: Meta-analysis revealing how conceptual metaphors structure intelligence theories — demonstrated multiplicity of incompatible intelligence frameworks

Intelligence Measurement And Psychometri.title: General Intelligence Objectively Determined and Measured
Intelligence Measurement And Psychometri.abstract: Spearman's foundational psychometric work establishing the two-factor theory of intelligence (general intelligence 'g' plus specific abilities), creating the scientific basis for measuring intelligence as a unified cognitive capacity
Intelligence Measurement And Psychometri.seminal_reason: Established general intelligence (g) as a measurable construct, founding modern psychometrics of intelligence

Intelligence Measurement And Psychometri.title: The Intelligence Test Desk Reference (ITDR): Gf-Gc Cross-Battery Assessment
Intelligence Measurement And Psychometri.authors_str: Kevin S. McGrew; Dawn P. Flanagan
Intelligence Measurement And Psychometri.abstract: Comprehensive reference integrating Cattell-Horn-Carroll (Gf-Gc) theory of intelligence with practical assessment methods, establishing modern hierarchical framework for understanding and measuring intelligence
Intelligence Measurement And Psychometri.seminal_reason: Synthesized Gf-Gc hierarchical intelligence theory with assessment practice

Intelligence Measurement Controversy.title: General Intelligence Objectively Determined and Measured
Intelligence Measurement Controversy.abstract: Spearman proposed the two-factor theory of intelligence, introducing the concept of 'g' (general intelligence factor) underlying all cognitive abilities — established psychometric approach to intelligence as a unitary, measurable capacity
Intelligence Measurement Controversy.seminal_reason: Introduced g-factor theory and statistical foundations of psychometric intelligence — established intelligence as a general, quantifiable cognitive capacity

Intelligence Measurement Controversy.title: The Social Construction of Facts and Artefacts: or How the Sociology of Science and the Sociology of Technology might Benefit Each Other
Intelligence Measurement Controversy.abstract: Established social constructivism as a framework for understanding both scientific facts and technological artifacts as products of social negotiation rather than objective discoveries — relevant because intelligence itself may be a socially constructed category rather than a natural kind
Intelligence Measurement Controversy.seminal_reason: Established empirical program for studying scientific knowledge as social construction — foundational for questioning whether intelligence is discovered or constructed

Intelligence Measurement Controversy.title: Handbook of Competence and Motivation
Intelligence Measurement Controversy.authors_str: Andrew J. Elliot; Carol S. Dweck
Intelligence Measurement Controversy.abstract: Comprehensive handbook examining competence and motivation from multiple theoretical perspectives including intelligence (Sternberg), implicit motives (Schultheiss/Brunstein), and competence as core of achievement motivation
Intelligence Measurement Controversy.seminal_reason: Synthesized competence and motivation research positioning competence (related to intelligence) as core motivational construct

Intentionality And Mental Content.title: Intentionality
Intentionality And Mental Content.abstract: Searle's influential work on intentionality—the property of mental states being 'about' or 'directed at' objects and states of affairs—establishing foundations for understanding how minds represent reality
Intentionality And Mental Content.seminal_reason: Established modern philosophical framework for intentionality and mental representation
intersection_tags: Intentionality and Mental Content x Consciousness and Self-Awareness

Intentionality And Mental Content.title: The Intentional Stance
Intentionality And Mental Content.abstract: Dennett's theory that we interpret systems (including ourselves) by attributing beliefs, desires, and rational agency—establishing a framework for understanding how intentional interpretation shapes cognition
Intentionality And Mental Content.seminal_reason: Established intentional stance as interpretive framework for understanding minds and agency
intersection_tags: Intentionality and Mental Content x Consciousness and Self-Awareness

Intentionality And Mental States.title: Intentionality
Intentionality And Mental States.abstract: Systematic treatment of intentionality as the mind's directedness toward objects and states of affairs, establishing that mental states have intrinsic intentionality while symbolic representations (like computer programs) have only derived intentionality—foundational to Chinese Room argument against strong AI.
Intentionality And Mental States.seminal_reason: Established intrinsic vs. derived intentionality distinction—core argument that AI systems lack genuine mental states.
intersection_tags: Intentionality and Mental States x Consciousness

Intentionality And Mental States.title: The Intentional Stance
Intentionality And Mental States.abstract: Argued that intentionality is a matter of taking an interpretive stance toward systems (physical, design, or intentional) for predictive purposes—whether attributed to humans, animals, or machines—challenging essentialist views of mental states and opening door to functional accounts of AI cognition.
Intentionality And Mental States.seminal_reason: Functionalist account allowing intentional attribution to AI—direct philosophical counter to Searle's intrinsic intentionality requirement.
intersection_tags: Intentionality and Mental States x Prediction and Control

Intentionality And Mental States.title: Views into the Chinese Room: New Essays on Searle and Artificial Intelligence
Intentionality And Mental States.abstract: Collection of essays examining Searle's Chinese Room argument and its implications for strong AI, with contributions defending and critiquing the argument that syntax (symbol manipulation) cannot generate semantics (meaning)—comprehensive treatment of central debate in AI philosophy.
Intentionality And Mental States.seminal_reason: Comprehensive examination of Chinese Room argument—central debate on whether AI can possess genuine understanding.
intersection_tags: Intentionality and Mental States x Intelligence as Abstraction

Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficie.title: Foreign Agricultural Economic Report series (United States Department of Agriculture. Economic Research Service)
authors: United States Department of Agriculture. Economic Research Service
Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficie.authors_str: United States Department of Agriculture. Economic Research Service
Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficie.abstract: Systematic government reports analyzing agricultural economics across countries, establishing comparative frameworks for production systems
Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficie.seminal_reason: Institutionalized cross-national agricultural comparison, creating data infrastructure for scale and system comparisons
categories: Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficiency
Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficie.primary_category: Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficiency
theme_tags: land use and resource allocation efficiency

Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficie.title: Global Agro-ecological Assessment for Agriculture in the 21st Century: Methodology and Results
Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficie.abstract: Developed the agro-ecological zones (AEZ) methodology for assessing land suitability and agricultural potential globally, creating spatial frameworks for production analysis
Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficie.seminal_reason: Established methodological foundations for spatial and ecological assessment of agricultural systems — enabling context-specific comparisons
categories: Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficiency
Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficie.primary_category: Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficiency
theme_tags: land use and resource allocation efficiency

Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficie.title: Concepts of agricultural marginal lands and their utilisation: A review
Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficie.abstract: Traced evolution of 'marginal land' concept, showing how definitions shifted from biophysical to socio-economic and back, revealing contested boundaries of agricultural land
Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficie.seminal_reason: Demonstrated that 'marginal' is historically contingent, not fixed — relevant to understanding how scale and viability are defined
categories: Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficiency
Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficie.primary_category: Land Use And Resource Allocation Efficiency
theme_tags: land use and resource allocation efficiency

Meaning And Human Flourishing.title: Man's Search for Meaning
Meaning And Human Flourishing.abstract: Holocaust survivor's account of finding meaning through suffering, establishing logotherapy and the foundational principle that meaning-making is central to human existence—that humans need purpose, not just pleasure or power—foundational to existential psychology.
Meaning And Human Flourishing.seminal_reason: Established meaning-seeking as core human need—normative foundation for evaluating AI's place relative to human purpose.
intersection_tags: Meaning and Human Flourishing x Autonomy and Agency

Mechanical Vs Organic Intelligence.title: A History of Transhumanist Thought
Mechanical Vs Organic Intelligence.abstract: Bostrom's intellectual history of human enhancement from ancient myths through Enlightenment perfectibility to modern transhumanism. Traced perennial desire to transcend natural human cognitive limitations. Documented how technology reopened questions about fixed human nature and intelligence boundaries.
Mechanical Vs Organic Intelligence.seminal_reason: Documented historical continuity from ancient enhancement dreams to contemporary AI debates, showing perennial human desire to transcend natural intelligence limits
intersection_tags: Divine vs Human Intelligence x Mechanical vs Organic Intelligence

Mechanism And Mind.title: Discourse on Method
Mechanism And Mind.abstract: Establishes rational method and positions human reason as universal instrument applicable to all domains. Distinguishes human language and flexible reason from animal mechanical response, creating sharp human-animal cognitive boundary.
Mechanism And Mind.seminal_reason: Introduced language test for intelligence and hardened human-animal divide based on rational flexibility versus mechanical determination

Mechanism And Mind.title: Meditations on First Philosophy
Mechanism And Mind.abstract: Establishes mind-body dualism: thinking substance (res cogitans) versus extended substance (res extensa). Intelligence as immaterial, non-spatial capacity for clear and distinct ideas. Animals as automata lacking rational soul.
Mechanism And Mind.seminal_reason: Revolutionary rupture positioning intelligence outside mechanical Nature entirely — established modern problem of how immaterial mind relates to material world

Mechanism And Mind.title: An Essay Concerning Human Understanding
Mechanism And Mind.abstract: Empiricist account of intelligence as acquired through sensory experience. No innate ideas — mind as blank slate (tabula rasa). Understanding as combination and abstraction from simple ideas. Degrees of intelligence based on associative capacity.
Mechanism And Mind.seminal_reason: Naturalized intelligence as empirical psychological capacity rather than metaphysical substance — opened intelligence to scientific study and individual variation

Mechanism And Mind.title: Critique of Pure Reason
Mechanism And Mind.abstract: Distinguishes understanding (Verstand) as rule-applying faculty from reason (Vernunft) as faculty of systematic unity and regulative ideas. Sensibility, understanding, and reason as three distinct cognitive capacities. Human intelligence limited to phenomenal realm.
Mechanism And Mind.seminal_reason: Most systematic modern categorization of cognitive faculties — distinguished types of intelligence by logical function rather than metaphysical substance

Mechanism And Mind.title: The Conscious Mind: In Search of a Fundamental Theory
Mechanism And Mind.abstract: Distinguishes hard problem of consciousness from easy problems of cognitive function. Intelligence as functional capacity separable from phenomenal consciousness. Property dualism — consciousness as fundamental feature of nature.
Mechanism And Mind.seminal_reason: Sharpened distinction between functional intelligence and phenomenal consciousness — repositioned consciousness within natural ontology

Medieval Intellect Synthesis.title: De Trinitate (On the Trinity)
Medieval Intellect Synthesis.abstract: Augustine's synthesis of Neoplatonism and Christianity. Introduces divine illumination theory — human intelligence requires direct divine light to grasp eternal truths. Memory, understanding, and will as image of Trinity in human soul.
Medieval Intellect Synthesis.seminal_reason: Established divine illumination as requirement for human knowledge, positioning human intelligence as necessarily dependent on and inferior to divine intelligence

Medieval Intellect Synthesis.title: Kitab al-Shifa (The Book of Healing)
Medieval Intellect Synthesis.abstract: Comprehensive philosophical encyclopedia synthesizing Aristotelian and Neoplatonic thought. Distinguishes agent intellect (Active Intelligence) as separate celestial intelligence from human material and habitual intellect. Human intelligence actualized through illumination from Agent Intellect.
Medieval Intellect Synthesis.seminal_reason: Introduced agent intellect as separate cosmic intelligence, fundamentally repositioning human intelligence as receptive rather than self-sufficient — transmitted to Latin West

Medieval Intellect Synthesis.title: Tahafut al-Falasifa (The Incoherence of the Philosophers)
Medieval Intellect Synthesis.abstract: Critique of Aristotelian philosophy from Islamic theological perspective. Challenges philosophical claims about intellect's self-sufficiency and necessity of natural causation. Positions human intelligence as limited and dependent on divine revelation.
Medieval Intellect Synthesis.seminal_reason: Major challenge to Greek rationalist confidence in human intelligence, establishing tension between philosophical reason and revealed wisdom that shaped medieval debates

Medieval Intellect Synthesis.title: Guide for the Perplexed (Dalalat al-Ha'irin)
Medieval Intellect Synthesis.abstract: Synthesis of Aristotelian philosophy and Jewish theology. Distinguishes four levels of intellect: potential, habitual, actual, and acquired. Positions prophecy as highest actualization of human intellect through conjunction with Agent Intellect.
Medieval Intellect Synthesis.seminal_reason: Systematic hierarchy of intelligence levels from potentiality to prophetic actualization, integrating Greek categories with Abrahamic revelation framework

Medieval Intellect Synthesis.title: Summa Theologiae
Medieval Intellect Synthesis.abstract: Comprehensive synthesis of Aristotelian philosophy and Christian theology. Distinguishes angelic intelligence (intuitive, non-discursive), human intelligence (discursive, abstractive), and animal estimative power. Human intelligence as highest natural capacity subordinate to angels and God.
Medieval Intellect Synthesis.seminal_reason: Most influential medieval categorization of intelligence hierarchy: divine, angelic, human rational, animal sensitive — framework dominating Western thought until modernity

Mind-Body Problem.title: Parts of Animals
Mind-Body Problem.abstract: Aristotle's teleological analysis of animal anatomy and physiology, explaining how bodily structures serve functions including cognitive capacities. Connected physical organization to psychic capabilities.
Mind-Body Problem.seminal_reason: Established hylomorphic framework connecting bodily form to psychic function — precursor to embodied cognition theories

Mind-Body Problem.title: Parva naturalia
Mind-Body Problem.abstract: Collection of short Aristotelian treatises on sense perception, memory, sleep, dreams, and related psycho-physical phenomena. Investigated the physiological basis of cognitive functions.
Mind-Body Problem.seminal_reason: Pioneered psycho-physical investigation of cognitive functions, establishing framework for understanding intelligence as embodied phenomenon
intersection_tags: Mind-Body Problem x Soul and Intellect

Mind-Body Problem.title: Meditations on First Philosophy
Mind-Body Problem.abstract: Descartes's foundational work establishing modern rationalism through methodological doubt. Argued mind (res cogitans) is essentially thinking substance completely distinct from body (res extensa). Positioned human intellect as finite thinking substance created by infinite divine intellect, with innate ideas implanted by God. Reduced animal behavior to mechanical automatism without genuine intelligence or consciousness.
Mind-Body Problem.seminal_reason: Established substance dualism and mechanical philosophy, radically separating human rational soul from animal mechanisms and inaugurating modern mind-body problem
intersection_tags: Mind-Body Problem x Mechanical vs Organic Intelligence

Mind-Body Problem.title: Ethics
Mind-Body Problem.abstract: Spinoza's rationalist metaphysical system presented geometrically. Rejected Cartesian dualism, arguing mind and body are parallel attributes of single substance (God/Nature). Positioned human intellect as finite mode of infinite divine intellect, with human knowledge progressing from imagination through reason to intuitive scientia. Human intelligence participates in God's intelligence as finite expression of infinite thought-attribute.
