Mind-Body Problem.seminal_reason: Dissolved mind-body dualism into dual-aspect monism, making human intelligence finite mode of God's infinite thought rather than separate substance
intersection_tags: Mind-Body Problem x Divine vs Human Intelligence

Mind-Body Problem.title: Being and Time
Mind-Body Problem.abstract: Heidegger's phenomenological ontology analyzing human existence (Dasein) as Being-in-the-world. Rejected Cartesian subject-object dualism and intellect as detached observer. Positioned understanding as primordial existential structure, not cognitive faculty. Human intelligence grounded in practical engagement and disclosure of meaningful contexts before theoretical cognition.
Mind-Body Problem.seminal_reason: Displaced theoretical intellect from primary position, making practical worldly engagement foundational to human understanding
intersection_tags: Mind-Body Problem x Embodied and Extended Mind

Mind-Brain Identity Theory.title: Is Consciousness a Brain Process?
Mind-Brain Identity Theory.abstract: Place's pioneering argument that consciousness is identical to brain processes, initiating the mind-brain identity theory movement in philosophy of mind
Mind-Brain Identity Theory.seminal_reason: First major defense of mind-brain identity theory in 20th century analytic philosophy
intersection_tags: Mind-Brain Identity Theory x Consciousness and Self-Awareness

Mind-Brain Identity Theory.title: The Mind-brain Identity Theory
Mind-Brain Identity Theory.abstract: Comprehensive collection of foundational papers on mind-brain identity theory, documenting the central-state materialism position that mental states are identical to brain states
Mind-Brain Identity Theory.seminal_reason: Collected foundational papers establishing type-identity and token-identity positions in philosophy of mind
intersection_tags: Mind-Brain Identity Theory x Consciousness and Self-Awareness

Mind-Brain Identity Theory.title: Philosophy of Mind
Mind-Brain Identity Theory.abstract: Kim's comprehensive textbook on philosophy of mind, examining mind-body materialism, physicalism, mental causation, and varieties of mental phenomena—establishing core conceptual framework for relating mind to physical reality
Mind-Brain Identity Theory.seminal_reason: Authoritative synthesis of philosophy of mind establishing core problems and positions
intersection_tags: Mind-Brain Identity Theory x Consciousness and Self-Awareness
Mind-Brain Identity Theory x Intentionality and Mental Content

Mind-Brain Identity Theory.title: The Blackwell Guide to Philosophy of Mind
Mind-Brain Identity Theory.authors_str: Stephen P. Stich; Ted A. Warfield
Mind-Brain Identity Theory.abstract: Comprehensive handbook covering major topics in philosophy of mind including consciousness, intentionality, mental causation, and the nature of mental states
Mind-Brain Identity Theory.seminal_reason: Authoritative handbook establishing contemporary philosophy of mind landscape
intersection_tags: Mind-Brain Identity Theory x Consciousness and Self-Awareness x Intentionality and Mental Content

Mind-Brain Identity Theory.title: Mental Causation and the Metaphysics of Mind
Mind-Brain Identity Theory.abstract: Collection examining how mental states bring about physical behavior, addressing the fundamental problem of mental causation since Descartes's mind-body division
Mind-Brain Identity Theory.seminal_reason: Comprehensive treatment of mental causation problem central to mind-body relationship
intersection_tags: Mind-Brain Identity Theory x Consciousness and Self-Awareness

Mind-Brain Identity Theory.title: Mind and Causality
Mind-Brain Identity Theory.abstract: Examination of causal patterns in mental processes and mechanisms of cognitive self-organization, addressing naturalistic accounts of intentionality while avoiding reductive materialism
Mind-Brain Identity Theory.seminal_reason: Addressed causal mechanisms linking mind to physical processes without reduction
intersection_tags: Mind-Brain Identity Theory x Intentionality and Mental Content

Mind-Brain Identity Theory.title: Neither Brain nor Ghost
Mind-Brain Identity Theory.abstract: Critique of both mind-brain identity theory and dualism, proposing embodied and embedded accounts of mind that reject traditional mind-body dichotomies
Mind-Brain Identity Theory.seminal_reason: Challenged both identity theory and dualism with embodied cognition approach
intersection_tags: Mind-Brain Identity Theory x Consciousness and Self-Awareness

Multiple Forms Of Intelligence.title: Frames of Mind: The Theory of Multiple Intelligences
Multiple Forms Of Intelligence.abstract: Revolutionary theory proposing that intelligence is not a single general capacity but comprises at least seven distinct forms (linguistic, logical-mathematical, spatial, musical, bodily-kinesthetic, interpersonal, intrapersonal)—challenged monolithic conceptions of intelligence and IQ testing paradigm.
Multiple Forms Of Intelligence.seminal_reason: Shattered unitary intelligence model—raised question of which intelligences AI can embody and which remain human.
intersection_tags: Multiple Forms of Intelligence x Human vs. Animal Cognition

Narrative Identity.title: Poetics
Narrative Identity.abstract: Ancient Greek foundational text on narrative structure and meaning-making through storytelling, establishing intellectual foundations for how humans construct coherent accounts of events and character
Narrative Identity.seminal_reason: Established narrative as fundamental to human meaning-making, foundational for later narrative identity theory
intersection_tags: Narrative Identity x Personal Identity and Continuity

Narrative Identity.title: Narrative Identity
Narrative Identity.abstract: First full theoretical model of narrative identity, proposing that individuals construct identity through internalized life stories that integrate past, present, and future into coherent meaning
Narrative Identity.seminal_reason: Established narrative identity as a theoretical framework, founding the narrative approach to selfhood
intersection_tags: Narrative Identity x Personal Identity and Continuity

Narrative Identity.title: The Routledge International Handbook on Narrative and Life History
Narrative Identity.abstract: Comprehensive handbook documenting the substantial turn toward narrative and life history study across disciplines, establishing narrative approaches to identity construction and selfhood
Narrative Identity.seminal_reason: Documented interdisciplinary turn to narrative as central to identity construction
intersection_tags: Narrative Identity x Personal Identity and Continuity

Narrative Selfhood.title: Poetics
Narrative Selfhood.abstract: Though focused on dramatic theory, Aristotle's Poetics established principles of narrative identity, character development through action, and the unity of plot that anticipate modern narrative identity theories — characters are defined by their actions and choices forming coherent wholes
Narrative Selfhood.seminal_reason: Established narrative coherence and character identity as temporal unity of action — foundational for understanding identity through storytelling

Narrative Selfhood.title: Practical Identity and Narrative Agency
Narrative Selfhood.abstract: Edited volume addressing practical identity (Korsgaard's Kantian concept) and narrative approaches to agency, shifting focus from metaphysical questions about persistence to evaluative and practical concerns about who we take ourselves to be
Narrative Selfhood.seminal_reason: Established practical identity as distinct from metaphysical identity — shifted focus to evaluative self-conception and agency

Nature (Physis) And Natural Order.title: Physics
Nature (Physis) And Natural Order.source: referenced in discussions of physis and natural philosophy
Nature (Physis) And Natural Order.abstract: Aristotle's treatise on nature (physis) as the principle of motion and change in natural beings, establishing the framework for understanding natural substances and their inherent tendencies
Nature (Physis) And Natural Order.seminal_reason: Defined physis (nature) as internal principle of motion and rest—established the framework for understanding intelligence as naturally arising capacity versus externally imposed form

Nature (Physis) And Natural Order.title: On the Parts of Animals
Nature (Physis) And Natural Order.abstract: Aristotle's biological treatise examining functional organization of animal bodies according to final causes—establishing teleological framework for understanding intelligence distributed across living nature
Nature (Physis) And Natural Order.seminal_reason: Established teleological biology and functional analysis of living forms—foundational for understanding intelligence as natural capacity serving biological ends across species

Nature (Physis) And Natural Order.title: History of Animals
Nature (Physis) And Natural Order.abstract: Aristotle's comprehensive empirical survey of animal characteristics, behaviors, and classification—first systematic comparative study providing evidence for graduated intelligence across species
Nature (Physis) And Natural Order.seminal_reason: First systematic comparative zoology—provided empirical basis for graduated scala naturae with intelligence distributed hierarchically from simpler to more complex organisms

Nous And Rational Intellect.title: Commentary on Aristotle's De Anima
Nous And Rational Intellect.source: referenced in 'The Truth in Aristotle and Sophonias'
Nous And Rational Intellect.abstract: Byzantine Neoplatonic commentary on Aristotle's On the Soul interpreting nous and psyche through later Platonic frameworks—preserving and transmitting ancient debates on intelligence
Nous And Rational Intellect.seminal_reason: Byzantine preservation and Neoplatonic interpretation of Aristotelian psychology—transmitted ancient debates on nous and psyche to medieval and Renaissance thinkers

Personal Identity And Continuity.title: An Essay Concerning Human Understanding
Personal Identity And Continuity.abstract: Locke's seminal work establishing personal identity as constituted by continuity of consciousness and memory rather than bodily continuity, founding the psychological continuity tradition in personal identity theory
Personal Identity And Continuity.seminal_reason: Established memory and consciousness as the basis of personal identity, founding psychological approaches to selfhood
intersection_tags: Personal Identity and Continuity x Consciousness and Self-Awareness

Personal Identity And Continuity.title: Eight Stages of Man
Personal Identity And Continuity.abstract: Erikson's developmental stage theory establishing identity formation as a central psychosocial crisis of adolescence, integrating psychoanalytic theory with social and cultural factors in identity development
Personal Identity And Continuity.seminal_reason: Established identity formation as a developmental process with psychosocial stages, founding modern identity development theory
intersection_tags: Personal Identity and Continuity x Cognitive Development Theory
Social Identity and Group Membership x Cognitive Development Theory

Personal Identity And Continuity.title: Self and Identity
Personal Identity And Continuity.abstract: Comprehensive philosophical anthology on personal identity, collecting classical and contemporary arguments about what constitutes the self and identity over time
Personal Identity And Continuity.seminal_reason: Major collection establishing the contemporary philosophical landscape of personal identity debates
intersection_tags: Personal Identity and Continuity x Consciousness and Self-Awareness

Personal Identity And Continuity.title: Problems in Personal Identity
Personal Identity And Continuity.abstract: Systematic examination of major problems in personal identity theory, analyzing psychological continuity, bodily continuity, and criteria for identity persistence
Personal Identity And Continuity.seminal_reason: Synthesized major problems in personal identity, establishing core conceptual puzzles in the field
intersection_tags: Personal Identity and Continuity x Consciousness and Self-Awareness

Personal Identity And Continuity.title: A Value-Belief-Norm Theory of Support for Social Movements: The Case of Environmentalism
Personal Identity And Continuity.abstract: Theory establishing how personal values, beliefs about consequences, and ascribed responsibility create moral norms that shape action—linking identity-based values to cognitive-behavioral patterns
Personal Identity And Continuity.seminal_reason: Established value-belief-norm chain linking identity-based values to cognition and action
intersection_tags: Personal Identity and Continuity x Social Identity and Group Membership

Personal Identity And Continuity.title: Personal Identity and Self-Consciousness
Personal Identity And Continuity.abstract: Accessible philosophical examination of personal identity and self-consciousness, addressing metaphysical questions about what we are and value questions about why identity matters
Personal Identity And Continuity.seminal_reason: Synthesized personal identity metaphysics with value theory, linking identity to mattering
intersection_tags: Personal Identity and Continuity x Consciousness and Self-Awareness

Personal Identity And Continuity.title: Handbook of Eudaimonic Well-Being
Personal Identity And Continuity.abstract: Comprehensive account of eudaimonic well-being examining philosophical and empirical approaches to living well, linking identity, meaning, and human flourishing
Personal Identity And Continuity.seminal_reason: Synthesized philosophical and empirical approaches to well-being and human flourishing
intersection_tags: Personal Identity and Continuity x Cognitive Development Theory

Personal Identity Persistence.title: An Essay Concerning Human Understanding
Personal Identity Persistence.abstract: Locke's foundational work in epistemology and philosophy of mind introduced the modern concept of personal identity as continuity of consciousness rather than substance, establishing the framework for all subsequent debates about what constitutes the persistence of the self over time
Personal Identity Persistence.seminal_reason: First systematic philosophical account of personal identity as psychological continuity — established identity as a forensic concept tied to consciousness and memory rather than material or spiritual substance

Personal Identity Persistence.title: Varieties of Moral Personality: Ethics and Psychological Realism
Personal Identity Persistence.abstract: Flanagan and Whiting argued for 'minimal psychological realism' in ethical theory — moral theories must be constrained by actual human psychological capacities, establishing that normative questions about identity and agency require empirical psychology
Personal Identity Persistence.seminal_reason: Established principle of minimal psychological realism — moral and identity theories must be constrained by actual human psychology

Personal Identity Persistence.title: Encyclopedia of Identity
Personal Identity Persistence.authors_str: Ronald L. Jackson; Michael A. Hogg
Personal Identity Persistence.abstract: Comprehensive encyclopedia covering definitions, politics, manifestations, concepts, and ideas related to identity across disciplines — established definitional landscape for identity scholarship
Personal Identity Persistence.seminal_reason: Most comprehensive reference work on identity across disciplines — established baseline for what counts as identity scholarship

Personal Identity Persistence.title: Self to Self: Selected Essays
Personal Identity Persistence.abstract: Velleman's collected essays argue there is no single entity denoted by 'the self' but rather multiple functional roles served by self-representation — challenges unified theories of personal identity
Personal Identity Persistence.seminal_reason: Argued against unified self — claimed 'self' denotes multiple functional roles rather than single entity

Philosophical Identity Personal Continui.title: An Essay Concerning Human Understanding
Philosophical Identity Personal Continui.abstract: Locke's chapter 'On Identity and Diversity' (II.xxvii) established the memory criterion for personal identity—that continuity of consciousness through memory constitutes the self across time. This founded the psychological approach to identity that remains central to philosophical debates.
Philosophical Identity Personal Continui.seminal_reason: Established memory-based psychological continuity as the criterion for personal identity, founding the philosophical tradition that identity is constituted by mental states rather than substance

Philosophical Identity Personal Continui.title: Essays on the Intellectual Powers of Man
Philosophical Identity Personal Continui.abstract: Reid's Essay 3 on 'Memory' critiqued Locke's memory criterion through thought experiments (the brave officer paradox), establishing that identity cannot reduce to memory alone but requires underlying continuity. This launched the debate between reductionist and non-reductionist accounts of personal identity.
Philosophical Identity Personal Continui.seminal_reason: Critiqued Locke's memory criterion and established the philosophical problem of whether identity can be reduced to psychological relations or requires non-reducible metaphysical substrate

Philosophical Mind Body Identity.title: Philosophiae Naturalis Principia Mathematica
Philosophical Mind Body Identity.abstract: Newton's laws of motion and universal gravitation established the mechanistic worldview that would eventually inform materialist accounts of mind and identity — the Newtonian framework made it conceivable to ask whether mental processes could be reduced to physical mechanisms
Philosophical Mind Body Identity.seminal_reason: Established mechanistic natural philosophy that made mind-body identity theories conceptually possible by demonstrating mathematical laws governing physical processes

Philosophical Mind Body Identity.title: A Historical Introduction to the Philosophy of Mind
Philosophical Mind Body Identity.abstract: Historical survey of philosophy of mind from ancient to contemporary period, designed to make technical material accessible by tracing how current debates emerged from historical questions about mind, consciousness, and identity
Philosophical Mind Body Identity.seminal_reason: Comprehensive historical introduction showing how mind-brain identity theory emerged from earlier philosophical problems

Philosophical Mind Body Identity.title: Natural Minds
Philosophical Mind Body Identity.abstract: Polger defended mind-brain identity theory against multiple realization objections, arguing that sensations ARE brain processes — revived philosophical identity theory by arguing that mental states can be identified with specific neural states despite apparent variability
Philosophical Mind Body Identity.seminal_reason: Contemporary defense of mind-brain identity theory against multiple realization — revived philosophical identity theory by addressing its most serious objection

Philosophical Mind Body Identity.title: Neither Brain nor Ghost: A Nondualist Alternative to the Mind-Brain Identity Theory
Philosophical Mind Body Identity.abstract: Rockwell rejected both dualism and mind-brain identity theory, proposing that mental phenomena emerge from brain-body-world interactions rather than being localized in the brain — established extended mind as alternative to identity theory
Philosophical Mind Body Identity.seminal_reason: Established embodied-embedded alternative to mind-brain identity theory — argued mental phenomena emerge from brain-body-world nexus

Physis And Natural Order.title: Physics
Physis And Natural Order.abstract: Aristotle's foundational treatise on Nature (physis) as internal principle of motion and rest. Establishes teleological framework where natural beings possess inherent ends and degrees of perfection, creating hierarchical cosmos with rational soul at pinnacle of natural order.
Physis And Natural Order.seminal_reason: Defined physis as self-organizing principle and established natural hierarchy based on degree of form and actuality — framework persisting through medieval period

Prediction And Control.title: Long Short-Term Memory
Prediction And Control.abstract: Introduced LSTM architecture solving vanishing gradient problem in recurrent neural networks, enabling learning of long-term dependencies—foundational to modern deep learning and later attention mechanisms underlying large language models and contemporary AI systems.
Prediction And Control.seminal_reason: Solved fundamental neural network limitation—architectural foundation for modern LLMs and deep learning AI.
intersection_tags: Prediction and Control x Intelligence as Abstraction

Prediction And Control.title: Hierarchical Bayesian Inference in the Visual Cortex
Prediction And Control.authors_str: Tai Sing Lee; David B. Mumford
Prediction And Control.abstract: Proposed that visual cortex implements hierarchical Bayesian inference combining bottom-up sensory data with top-down generative models—established computational framework for understanding biological vision that influenced both neuroscience and AI computer vision systems.
Prediction And Control.seminal_reason: Established hierarchical Bayesian framework for vision—computational bridge between biological and artificial visual intelligence.
intersection_tags: Prediction and Control x Human vs. Animal Cognition

Prediction And Control.title: Whatever Next? Predictive Brains, Situated Agents, and the Future of Cognitive Science
Prediction And Control.abstract: Argued that brains are essentially prediction machines using hierarchical generative models to match incoming sensory inputs with top-down expectations—unified diverse cognitive phenomena under predictive processing framework and established fundamental architectural comparison point for AI systems.
Prediction And Control.seminal_reason: Established predictive processing as brain's fundamental architecture—reveals core similarity and difference with AI pattern recognition.
intersection_tags: Prediction and Control x Embodiment and Situatedness

Psychological Measurement.title: The Measurement of Intelligence
Psychological Measurement.abstract: Stanford-Binet Intelligence Scale adaptation. Standardized IQ testing establishing intelligence as single quantifiable trait distributed normally in populations. Mental age versus chronological age measurement.
Psychological Measurement.seminal_reason: Established psychometric paradigm treating intelligence as unitary measurable quantity — dominated educational and psychological practice throughout 20th century

Psychological Measurement.title: The Nature of Intelligence and the Principles of Cognition
Psychological Measurement.abstract: Factor analysis establishing g-factor (general intelligence) and specific abilities. Intelligence as general mental energy underlying all cognitive tasks. Statistical methods for identifying intelligence structure.
Psychological Measurement.seminal_reason: Introduced factor-analytic approach and g-factor theory — established debate over unitary versus multiple intelligences dominating psychometrics

Psychometric Intelligence Structure.title: General Intelligence Objectively Determined and Measured
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.abstract: Spearman's first major article introduced factor analysis and the concept of general intelligence (g) as a single underlying capacity explaining correlations among diverse cognitive tests. This founded the psychometric tradition treating intelligence as a measurable, stable trait.
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.seminal_reason: Invented factor analysis and established 'g' (general intelligence) as the dominant psychometric construct, founding the scientific measurement tradition that treats intelligence as unitary and trait-like

Psychometric Intelligence Structure.title: The Measurement of Intelligence
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.abstract: Terman adapted Binet's intelligence scale for American use, creating the Stanford-Binet test and popularizing the Intelligence Quotient (IQ). This standardized intelligence testing and embedded psychometric measurement in educational and institutional practice.
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.seminal_reason: Standardized intelligence testing through the Stanford-Binet scale and popularized IQ measurement, establishing psychometric intelligence as institutionalized educational and social sorting mechanism

Psychometric Intelligence Structure.title: The Measurement of Intelligence: An Explanation of and a Complete Guide for the Use of the Stanford Revision and Extension of the Binet-Simon Intelligence Scale
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.abstract: Terman's complete manual for Stanford-Binet testing established standardized protocols for intelligence measurement, including age norms and IQ calculation. This institutionalized intelligence testing as educational gatekeeping and launched debates about heredity, class, and testing bias.
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.seminal_reason: Provided complete standardization of intelligence testing including norms and protocols, institutionalizing psychometric intelligence as tool for educational sorting and triggering enduring debates about testing bias

Psychometric Intelligence Structure.title: The Intelligence of School Children
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.abstract: Terman applied Stanford-Binet testing to school populations, establishing intelligence testing as educational practice. This embedded psychometric intelligence in institutional structures and created tracking systems based on measured IQ, with lasting consequences for educational equity.
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.seminal_reason: Applied standardized intelligence testing to educational settings, embedding psychometric measurement in institutional tracking systems and establishing intelligence testing as educational gatekeeping mechanism

Psychometric Intelligence Structure.title: The Nature of Intelligence
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.abstract: A symposium in Journal of Educational Psychology where leading psychologists (Terman, Thorndike, Thurstone, et al.) defined intelligence. Definitions ranged from 'ability to carry on abstract thinking' to 'power of good responses from the point of view of truth or fact.' This revealed early conceptual pluralism about intelligence.
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.seminal_reason: Early symposium revealing that even founding psychometricians lacked consensus on intelligence definition, establishing persistent conceptual ambiguity at the heart of intelligence research

Psychometric Intelligence Structure.title: The Abilities of Man: Their Nature and Measurement
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.abstract: Spearman's comprehensive treatise elaborated his two-factor theory (general intelligence 'g' plus specific abilities 's'). This established the mathematical and theoretical foundations for factor-analytic approaches to intelligence structure, influencing all subsequent psychometric models.
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.seminal_reason: Elaborated two-factor theory of intelligence (g + s factors) providing mathematical foundations for factor analysis and establishing the psychometric tradition of modeling intelligence structure through correlational methods

Psychometric Intelligence Structure.title: Primary Mental Abilities
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.abstract: Thurstone challenged Spearman's unitary g by demonstrating multiple independent factors (verbal comprehension, word fluency, number facility, spatial visualization, associative memory, perceptual speed, reasoning). This established pluralist models of intelligence structure and launched the g vs. multiple abilities debate.
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.seminal_reason: Challenged Spearman's unitary g by identifying multiple independent primary mental abilities, establishing pluralist psychometric models and launching enduring debate between unitary and multiple intelligence theories

Psychometric Intelligence Structure.title: The Organization of Behavior: A Neuropsychological Theory
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.abstract: Hebb proposed that intelligence arises from neural organization shaped by experience. His 'cell assembly' theory linked psychological processes to neurological mechanisms, bridging brain and cognition. This founded the tradition of grounding intelligence in neural plasticity and learning.
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.seminal_reason: Proposed that intelligence emerges from experientially shaped neural organization (cell assemblies), founding neuroscientific approaches to intelligence that ground cognition in plastic neural mechanisms rather than fixed capacity

Psychometric Intelligence Structure.title: A Study of Thinking
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.authors_str: Jerome S. Bruner; Jacqueline J. Goodnow; George A. Austin
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.abstract: Bruner et al. founded cognitive psychology by studying concept formation and categorization strategies. This shifted focus from behavioral responses to internal mental processes, establishing cognition as active construction rather than passive reception.
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.seminal_reason: Founded cognitive psychology by demonstrating that thinking involves active strategies for organizing information, shifting psychology from behaviorism to study of internal mental representations and processes

Psychometric Intelligence Structure.title: The Measurement and Appraisal of Adult Intelligence
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.abstract: Wechsler's comprehensive text established his Verbal-Performance IQ model and defined intelligence as 'the aggregate or global capacity of the individual to act purposefully, to think rationally, and to deal effectively with his environment.' This emphasized functional, adaptive aspects over pure cognition.
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.seminal_reason: Defined intelligence as aggregate capacity for purposeful, rational action in environment rather than pure cognitive ability, establishing functional-adaptive approach and Verbal-Performance IQ distinction

Psychometric Intelligence Structure.title: Frames of Mind: The Theory of Multiple Intelligences
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.abstract: Gardner proposed seven distinct intelligences (linguistic, logical-mathematical, musical, bodily-kinesthetic, spatial, interpersonal, intrapersonal) based on neuropsychological, developmental, and cross-cultural evidence. This challenged psychometric g and argued intelligence is domain-specific and culturally embedded.
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.seminal_reason: Challenged unitary psychometric intelligence by proposing multiple independent intelligences grounded in neuroscience and cultural practices, establishing radical pluralist alternative to g theory

Psychometric Intelligence Structure.title: The intelligence test desk reference (ITDR): Gf-Gc cross-battery assessment
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.authors_str: Kevin S. McGrew; Dawn P. Flanagan
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.abstract: McGrew and Flanagan provided comprehensive synthesis of Cattell-Horn-Carroll (CHC) theory—the integrated model combining Cattell's fluid-crystallized intelligence, Horn's elaboration, and Carroll's three-stratum model. This established CHC as the consensus psychometric framework organizing broad and narrow cognitive abilities.
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.seminal_reason: Synthesized Cattell-Horn-Carroll theory as consensus psychometric framework organizing intelligence into hierarchical structure of broad and narrow abilities, establishing standard taxonomy for contemporary intelligence assessment

Psychometric Intelligence Structure.title: The Cattell-Horn-Carroll Theory of Cognitive Abilities
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.authors_str: Dawn P. Flanagan; Shauna G. Dixon
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.abstract: Flanagan and Dixon describe CHC theory as the most comprehensive and empirically supported psychometric theory of cognitive abilities, integrating Cattell's Gf-Gc theory, Horn's elaboration, and Carroll's three-stratum model into hierarchical framework with general intelligence, broad abilities, and narrow abilities.
Psychometric Intelligence Structure.seminal_reason: Established CHC theory as most comprehensive psychometric model organizing intelligence into three-stratum hierarchy (general, broad, narrow abilities), synthesizing major factor-analytic traditions

Role Identity Commitment.title: The Nature of the Firm
Role Identity Commitment.abstract: Though focused on institutional economics, Coase's work on transaction costs and organizational boundaries established foundational concepts about how identity boundaries are maintained and why certain activities are organized under unified identities — relevant for understanding identity as organizational principle
Role Identity Commitment.seminal_reason: Established transaction cost theory explaining why bounded identities (firms) exist rather than continuous markets — foundational for understanding identity as functional boundary

Role Identity Commitment.title: Notes on the Concept of Commitment
Role Identity Commitment.abstract: Becker introduced the 'side-bet' theory of commitment — people become locked into consistent lines of action when extraneous interests become staked on maintaining that identity, establishing a mechanism for how identity commitments constrain behavior and potentially cognitive engagement
Role Identity Commitment.seminal_reason: Introduced side-bet theory explaining how identity commitments are maintained through accumulated investments — foundational for understanding identity as behavioral constraint

Rural Development And Smallholder Liveli.title: The rural non-farm sector: issues and evidence from developing countries
Rural Development And Smallholder Liveli.abstract: Challenged the view that rural non-farm sectors are low-productivity residuals, recognizing their economic importance and integration with agricultural systems
Rural Development And Smallholder Liveli.seminal_reason: Expanded understanding of rural economies beyond pure agriculture, contextualizing small-scale farming within broader livelihood systems

Self Concept Development.title: Human Nature and the Social Order
Self Concept Development.abstract: Cooley introduced the 'looking-glass self' concept — the idea that self-concept arises from imagining how others perceive us and internalizing those reflected appraisals, establishing a foundational mechanism for how social context shapes identity
Self Concept Development.seminal_reason: Introduced the looking-glass self mechanism — first systematic account of how identity is constructed through reflected appraisals from others

Self Concept Development.title: Mind, Self and Society
Self Concept Development.abstract: Mead's foundational work in symbolic interactionism established that the self emerges through social interaction and role-taking, not as a pre-existing entity but as a reflexive process arising from communication with others — the 'I' and 'me' distinction became central to understanding identity as socially constructed
Self Concept Development.seminal_reason: Established symbolic interactionism and the social genesis of self — foundational for understanding identity as emergent from social processes rather than innate essence

Self Concept Development.title: Development Through the Lifespan
Self Concept Development.abstract: Comprehensive developmental psychology textbook establishing how identity formation and cognitive development interact across the lifespan — bridged cognitive and social-emotional development
Self Concept Development.seminal_reason: Comprehensive synthesis of developmental psychology showing identity and cognition as intertwined developmental processes

Self Concept Development.title: Moral Identity and Developmental Theory
Self Concept Development.abstract: Lapsley traced philosophical and psychological roots of the claim that self-identity and morality are deeply implicated, drawing on Frankfurt's account of personhood and examining how moral identity develops across the lifespan
Self Concept Development.seminal_reason: Established developmental account of moral identity drawing on Frankfurt's personhood theory — linked identity to what persons care about

Social Epistemology And Standpoint Theor.title: Situated Knowledges: The Science Question in Feminism and the Privilege of Partial Perspective
Social Epistemology And Standpoint Theor.abstract: Haraway's feminist epistemology arguing that all knowledge is situated from particular embodied positions, establishing that identity location shapes what can be known and how
Social Epistemology And Standpoint Theor.seminal_reason: Established situated knowledges—that identity position constitutes epistemic standpoint
intersection_tags: Social Epistemology and Standpoint Theory x Social Identity and Group Membership

Social Epistemology And Standpoint Theor.title: Black Feminist Thought: Knowledge, Consciousness, and the Politics of Empowerment
Social Epistemology And Standpoint Theor.abstract: Collins's foundational work in Black feminist epistemology establishing that racial and gender identity position creates distinctive standpoints that shape knowledge production, consciousness, and intellectual traditions
Social Epistemology And Standpoint Theor.seminal_reason: Established standpoint epistemology showing how identity categories constitute epistemic positions
intersection_tags: Social Epistemology and Standpoint Theory x Social Identity and Group Membership
Social Epistemology and Standpoint Theory x Consciousness and Self-Awareness

Social Identity And Group Membership.title: Social Identity Theory
Social Identity And Group Membership.abstract: Foundational theory establishing that individuals derive self-concept and meaning from group memberships, creating the psychological basis for understanding how social categories shape cognition and behavior
Social Identity And Group Membership.seminal_reason: Established social identity as derived from group membership, founding social identity tradition in psychology
intersection_tags: Social Identity and Group Membership x Personal Identity and Continuity

Social Identity And Group Membership.title: Self-Categorization Theory
Social Identity And Group Membership.abstract: Extension of social identity theory explaining how individuals categorize themselves into social groups and how this self-categorization shapes perception, cognition, and behavior
Social Identity And Group Membership.seminal_reason: Established mechanisms by which social categorization shapes cognitive processes
intersection_tags: Social Identity and Group Membership x Consciousness and Self-Awareness

Social Identity And Group Membership.title: Rethinking Racism: Toward a Structural Interpretation
Social Identity And Group Membership.abstract: Structural theory of racism arguing that racial inequality is maintained through social systems rather than individual prejudice, establishing how racial identity categories become institutionally embedded in cognitive and social structures
Social Identity And Group Membership.seminal_reason: Established structural racism theory showing how racial categories shape social-cognitive systems
intersection_tags: Social Identity and Group Membership x Social Epistemology and Standpoint Theory

Social Identity And Group Membership.title: The Quest for Collective Identity: The Plight of Disadvantaged Ethnic Minorities
Social Identity And Group Membership.abstract: Analysis of how disadvantaged ethnic minorities experience profound social problems including academic underachievement linked to collective identity struggles and social marginalization
Social Identity And Group Membership.seminal_reason: Established link between ethnic minority identity and academic/cognitive underachievement
intersection_tags: Social Identity and Group Membership x Intelligence Measurement and Psychometrics

Social Identity And Group Membership.title: Women and Human Development
Social Identity And Group Membership.abstract: Nussbaum's capabilities approach arguing for universal human capabilities that constitute the basis for human flourishing, establishing normative framework linking identity, development, and human cognitive-moral capacities
Social Identity And Group Membership.seminal_reason: Established capabilities approach linking human identity and development to universal capacities
intersection_tags: Social Identity and Group Membership x Cognitive Development Theory

Social Identity And Group Membership.title: Identity and Interaction: A Sociocultural Linguistic Approach
Social Identity And Group Membership.abstract: Framework analyzing identity as produced in linguistic interaction rather than as internal essence, establishing identity as emergent from social and cultural semiotic practices
Social Identity And Group Membership.seminal_reason: Established identity as interactionally produced through linguistic practice, not internal essence
intersection_tags: Social Identity and Group Membership x Narrative Identity

Social Identity And Group Membership.title: Toward an African Moral Theory
Social Identity And Group Membership.abstract: Articulation and justification of African moral norms based on communal identity and relationality, establishing non-Western philosophical foundations for identity-based ethics and cognition
Social Identity And Group Membership.seminal_reason: Established African communal identity as foundation for moral theory, challenging Western individualism
intersection_tags: Social Identity and Group Membership x Personal Identity and Continuity

Social Identity And Group Membership.title: Encyclopedia of Identity
Social Identity And Group Membership.abstract: Comprehensive two-volume encyclopedia exploring identity as socially constructed phenomenon, documenting myriad ways individuals define themselves through dynamic public and private processes
Social Identity And Group Membership.seminal_reason: Comprehensive documentation of identity as socially constructed across multiple dimensions

Stereotype Threat Identity Performance.abstract: Steele and Aronson demonstrated that activating negative stereotypes about African American intelligence impairs test performance. Symbolic Interactionism Self Society.title: Mind, Self and Society
Symbolic Interactionism Self Society.abstract: Mead's posthumously published lectures established that self emerges through social interaction—particularly through taking the role of the 'generalized other.' The self is not given but socially constructed through symbolic communication. This founded symbolic interactionism and the view that identity is fundamentally social.
Symbolic Interactionism Self Society.seminal_reason: Established that self is not innate but emerges through social interaction and role-taking, founding symbolic interactionism and the view that identity is socially constructed rather than individual essence

Symbolic Interactionism Self Society.title: From Mead to a Structural Symbolic Interactionism and Beyond
Symbolic Interactionism Self Society.abstract: Stryker reviews Mead's contributions and develops 'structural symbolic interactionism' that links identity to social structure. He argues identities are organized hierarchically by salience (importance to self), which predicts identity activation across situations. This bridged micro-level identity processes and macro-level social structures.
Symbolic Interactionism Self Society.seminal_reason: Developed structural symbolic interactionism linking identity processes to social structure, establishing identity salience hierarchy as mechanism connecting self-concept to situational behavior

Universal Vs Individual Intellect.title: Tafsir (Commentary on Aristotle's De Anima)
Universal Vs Individual Intellect.abstract: Averroes's radical commentary on Aristotle arguing for monopsychism — the doctrine that there exists only one eternal, separate agent intellect shared by all humans. Individual humans participate in this single intellect when thinking, but possess no individual immortal rational soul.

Universal Vs Individual Intellect.title: Summa Theologiae
Universal Vs Individual Intellect.abstract: Aquinas's systematic theological and philosophical synthesis, combining Aristotelian philosophy with Christian doctrine. Against Averroes, argued each human possesses individual rational soul that is form of body yet subsistent and immortal. Distinguished human discursive reasoning from angelic intuitive intellection from divine self-knowing. Positioned human intelligence as lowest intellectual substance, requiring sensory input for knowledge.
Universal Vs Individual Intellect.seminal_reason: Established Thomistic synthesis giving each human individual rational soul while maintaining hierarchical gradation from human discursive to angelic intuitive to divine intellect
